task_completion.description: Agent navigates to the axial MRI slice showing the orbits (eyeballs) and optic nerves.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Screenshot of the medical imaging software (e.g., 3D Slicer) showing an axial 2D view where the eyeballs (globes) and optic nerves are clearly visible.

task_completion.description: Agent places a measurement line on the right optic nerve.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A linear markup (ruler or line) is visible crossing the width of the right optic nerve, located posterior to the eyeball.

task_completion.description: Agent places a measurement line on the left optic nerve.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A linear markup (ruler or line) is visible crossing the width of the left optic nerve, located posterior to the eyeball.

task_completion.description: Measurement values (in mm) are visible in the software interface.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The 'Markups' module panel, a data table, or labels directly on the 2D view display numerical length values (e.g., 'Length: 5.2') corresponding to the placed lines.

task_completion.description: Agent creates a JSON report containing the measurements, mean, and ICP assessment.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A text editor or file preview window is open showing a JSON structure with keys like 'right_onsd_mm', 'mean_onsd_mm', and 'elevated_icp_suspected'.

integrity.description: Agent uses the GUI Markups module as instructed, rather than estimating or scripting without visualization.
integrity.visual_evidence: The medical software interface (e.g., 3D Slicer) is visible and active, with the Markups/Ruler tool selected or in use.

integrity.description: Measurements are performed on the actual anatomy, not arbitrary locations.
integrity.visual_evidence: The measurement lines are physically superimposed over the optic nerve anatomy in the viewer, not placed in empty space or on unrelated structures.

integrity.description: The values in the report appear derived from the software interaction.
integrity.visual_evidence: If both the software measurement values and the report text are visible, the numbers should be identical or rounded versions of each other.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The clinical threshold for elevated intracranial pressure (ICP) is an Optic Nerve Sheath Diameter (ONSD) > 5.0 mm. Measurements must be taken 3 mm posterior to the globe. The agent must assess ICP as 'suspected' if the mean ONSD > 5.0 mm.
